chapter_title.en: Chapter 19 – A tree of the Al-Harram

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/82)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/82)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) الرَّجُلُ يَدْخُلُ مَكَّةَ فَيَقْطَعُ مِنْ شَجَرِهَا قَالَ اقْطَعْ مَا كَانَ دَاخِلًا عَلَيْكَ وَ لَا تَقْطَعْ مَا لَمْ يَدْخُلْ مَنْزِلَكَ عَلَيْكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Nast, from Abu Jameela, from Is’haq Bin Yazeed who said,

‘I said to Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The man enters Makkah, so he cuts down from its trees’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Cut down what was entering upon you, and do not cut down what did not enter you house, upon you’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu Nasr from abu Jamilah from Ishaq ibn Yazid who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who enters Makkah and cuts off its trees. He (the Imam) said, ‘You can cut what has entered in your place, but you must not cut what has not entered your place.’”
